visual_features.overall_style: Clean, modern, minimally styled corporate layout with light resort branding. Strong use of flat color (blue) for brand elements, lots of white, and a straightforward, text-focused presentation suited for policy/terms content.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Primary hierarchy via typography, alignment, and spacing. The page title is the dominant element (large, bold, centered). Subsection headings (e.g., '1. General Conditions') are bold, left-aligned, and slightly larger than body text, creating clear sectional breaks. Navigation bars (blue contact strip and white main nav) form a strong top frame; active nav item highlights the current section. Body text is uniform, supporting scannability through numbered sections rather than heavy styling.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Generous top padding below the navigation and consistent vertical spacing between headings and paragraphs. Content is constrained to a centered column with ample left/right margins, creating comfortable reading width. Sections are separated by vertical spacing rather than borders, which keeps the page visually light and uncluttered.
unique_elements: Distinct chalet logo lockup (small illustrated chalet mark with stacked text) in the left of the main navigation bar.
Topmost full-width contact strip with phone and email, using icons and inline text for quick access.
Cart summary in the top-right navigation showing an icon plus '0 Items', integrated as part of the nav bar rather than a separate button.
Dropdown indicators in the main nav (e.g., for 'Booking' and 'eStore'), suggesting multi-level navigation while keeping primary nav minimal.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Likely a 12-column responsive grid (Bootstrap-style), with content constrained in a centered max-width container and nav elements arranged in columns (logo, nav links, cart) within the same grid.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Classic top-fixed (or top-stacked) header layout: slim full-width contact bar, then full-width main navigation bar, followed by a single centered content column using a standard document layout.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Top contact bar → main navigation → main content wrapper. Within the content: page title → introductory paragraphs → numbered sections with bold subheadings and paragraph text. Each section is stacked vertically in a linear document flow.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Header contact info is aligned horizontally with left/right distribution (phone/email left, potentially empty or balanced to the right). Main logo is left-aligned; nav links are horizontally centered; cart is right-aligned, establishing a balanced tri-part layout. In the content area, the heading is centered while all subsequent text (subheadings and paragraphs) is left-aligned for readability.
responsive_hints: Single-column content layout with generous side margins implies natural collapse to full-width text on smaller screens.
Top contact bar and main navigation use horizontal flex-like alignment; these will likely stack or convert to a hamburger menu on mobile.
Centered container suggests use of a max-width with auto margins, which is standard for responsive designs.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow. A full-width top contact strip runs across the viewport, followed by a full-width white navigation bar with logo on the left, menu in the center, and cart on the right. Below the nav, the main content is a single centered column. Inside that column, the large page title is centered, followed by left-aligned paragraphs. Subsections are arranged as bold numbered headings followed by multi-line text blocks in a continuous document-style flow.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Roughly the top 10–15% of the viewport is dedicated to the stacked headers (thin blue contact strip plus taller white nav bar). The remaining vertical space is the main content area, which uses a central column occupying about 60–70% of the viewport width, surrounded by 15–20% marginal whitespace on each side on desktop. Within the content column, headings have larger vertical margins, paragraphs have moderate line spacing, and there are no dense sidebars or extra columns competing for space.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately spacious, document-like layout. Text blocks are readable with ample line height and comfortable spacing between paragraphs. The page avoids visual crowding—no sidebars, no inline ads, and few decorative elements. Overall density is tailored to reading legal text: information-rich but visually uncluttered.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: Initial attention is drawn to the bright blue top contact bar, then to the logo and navigation directly beneath, forming a horizontal sweep from left (logo) through center (nav links) to right (cart). From there, the eye moves downward to the bold, centered page title, then transitions into a strong vertical reading flow down the left-aligned text. The numbered section headings create visual anchors, guiding the eye sequentially down the page.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit CTA buttons are visible on this screenshot, but based on the brand styling: buttons are likely flat, solid-filled rectangles with slightly rounded corners (about 4px radius), using the primary blue (#0095FF) for filled states and white or light backgrounds with blue text for secondary states. Text is likely medium to bold weight, centered, with horizontal padding around 16–24px and vertical padding around 8–12px. Shadows appear to be minimal or absent; emphasis is primarily through color contrast.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typically implemented as either a semantic or Label.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Cards are not shown on this particular page, but for an e-store with this visual language they would likely be simple white rectangles with subtle separation via light borders (#E0E0E0) or very soft shadows (0 2px 6px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)), 4px corner radius, and internal padding of 16–24px. The visual hierarchy inside a card would be a title (slightly bold), optional image on top, then description and price or action elements.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Forms are not visible but can be inferred: full-width inputs within the content column, with rectangular fields, 4px corner radius, light gray border (#CCCCCC), and white background. Focus states likely use blue border or subtle glow in the primary brand color. Labels are probably placed above inputs, left-aligned, with smaller but bold or semi-bold text. Overall form density would be moderate: vertical spacing of 12–16px between fields, creating a clean, non-cramped experience.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Main navigation: horizontal, full-width white bar. Left: logo. Center: inline text links with generous horizontal spacing (around 24–32px gap), using dark gray text. Some items have small down-arrow icons indicating dropdowns. Right: cart icon plus item count text. The active nav item (‘eStore’) appears in blue, likely with either a bottom border/underline or color change to match the primary blue. Text is medium-weight sans-serif, uppercase or title case. The top contact strip is another navigational pattern: slim blue bar with white icons and text, aligned horizontally, giving quick contact access.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Terms & Conditions content uses numbered section headings (e.g., '1. General Conditions', '2. Rental of Equipment') combined with sequential list-style paragraphs (1.1, 1.2, etc.). Items are spaced with modest vertical gaps (about 8–12px), no visible dividers or bullets beyond the numbering. Hierarchy is achieved by bolding section titles and keeping paragraph text regular weight. The result is a clean, legal-document-type list with easy scanning via numeric prefixes.

heading.visual_style: Bold geometric or neo-grotesque sans-serif with a relatively high x-height, clean curves, and fairly even stroke widths. Headings look modern and friendly rather than strictly corporate.

body.visual_style: Clean, neutral sans-serif optimized for readability, with balanced proportions and good legibility at small sizes.

spacing_system.component_padding: Navigation links: ~8–12px vertical, 16–24px horizontal. Content container: ~24–32px padding top/bottom relative to the viewport edges. Cards/forms (inferred): 16–24px internal padding on all sides.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major content sections (e.g., between title and first paragraph, between subsections) is around 24–32px, with 16px spacing between consecutive paragraphs.

interaction_hints: Navigation links likely change text color to primary blue and/or show an underline or bottom border on hover; the active state already uses blue to indicate the current page.
Cart icon and item count are likely clickable, with hover states increasing contrast or adding subtle background tint.
Buttons (where present) would benefit from simple transitions (e.g., 150–200ms color and background transitions) and slight darkening/lightening on hover to match the flat visual language.
Dropdown menus (for 'Booking' and 'eStore') probably appear on hover or click with a simple fade/slide-down animation, maintaining the clean, minimal aesthetic.
Links in the text body (e.g., emails, external links) likely use the primary blue color and underline on hover to signal interactivity clearly in the context of dense legal copy.
